https://design.adaptyvbio.com/.title: Protein Binder Design Competition – Round 3
https://design.adaptyvbio.com/.description: Design protein binders targeting disease-relevant targets. Top designs are synthesised and assayed by Adaptyv Bio's automated platform.
https://design.adaptyvbio.com/.how_to_apply: Submit sequences through the online portal. Designs are evaluated computationally, and top candidates proceed to experimental validation.
https://design.adaptyvbio.com/.requirements: Protein sequences in FASTA format. Must follow submission guidelines for chain length and complexity.

https://predictioncenter.org/casp16/.title: CASP16 – Critical Assessment of Protein Structure Prediction
https://predictioncenter.org/casp16/.location: Global – results presented in Cancun, Mexico
https://predictioncenter.org/casp16/.prizes: Recognition + invited talk at CASP conference
https://predictioncenter.org/casp16/.description: The biennial 'Olympics' of protein structure prediction. Groups submit models for experimentally determined but unpublished target structures.
https://predictioncenter.org/casp16/.how_to_apply: Register on the CASP website, download target sequences, submit predictions through the online server or via email.
https://predictioncenter.org/casp16/.requirements: Predict 3D coordinates for target proteins. Acceptable formats: PDB, mmCIF.

https://competition.igem.org/.title: iGEM Grand Jamboree 2025
https://competition.igem.org/.prizes: Gold / Silver / Bronze medals + Special Awards
https://competition.igem.org/.description: Annual international synthetic biology competition. Teams engineer biological systems and present at the Grand Jamboree in Paris.
https://competition.igem.org/.how_to_apply: Form a team, register on iGEM website, pay registration fee, document project on Wiki.
https://competition.igem.org/.requirements: Team wiki, presentation, poster, and working biological project.

https://cafa.iddo.org/.title: CAFA6 – Critical Assessment of Functional Annotation
https://cafa.iddo.org/.description: Community challenge to advance computational prediction of gene product functions across the tree of life.
https://cafa.iddo.org/.how_to_apply: Register on CAFA website, download benchmark sets, submit predictions.
https://cafa.iddo.org/.requirements: GO term predictions with confidence scores.

https://www.nyas.org/events/2025/hack-th.title: Hack the Genome 2025 – Computational Genomics Hackathon
https://www.nyas.org/events/2025/hack-th.description: Weekend hackathon bringing together life scientists, computational researchers, and data scientists to solve genome biology challenges.
https://www.nyas.org/events/2025/hack-th.how_to_apply: Apply online with CV and motivation statement. Teams can be pre-formed or assigned.
https://www.nyas.org/events/2025/hack-th.requirements: In-person or remote participation. Laptop required.

https://dreamchallenges.org/.title: DREAM Challenges – Open Systems Biology Problems
https://dreamchallenges.org/.description: Ongoing series of challenges to catalyse community efforts to advance systems biology and translational medicine.
https://dreamchallenges.org/.how_to_apply: Join Synapse platform, register for specific challenge, submit predictions.
https://dreamchallenges.org/.requirements: Synapse account. Coding skills in Python/R recommended.

https://www.kaggle.com/competitions/leas.title: Leash Bio – BELKA Small Molecule Binding Prediction
https://www.kaggle.com/competitions/leas.team_size: Individual or team of up to 5
https://www.kaggle.com/competitions/leas.description: Predict small molecule binding to three protein targets at unprecedented scale. 133M small molecules to explore.
https://www.kaggle.com/competitions/leas.how_to_apply: Register on Kaggle, join competition, download dataset, submit predictions.

https://www.embl.org/events/.title: EMBL Computational Biology Symposium – Poster Prize
https://www.embl.org/events/.description: Annual EMBL symposium with dedicated poster competition for early-career computational biologists.
https://www.embl.org/events/.how_to_apply: Submit abstract through conference portal. Indicate poster preference.
https://www.embl.org/events/.requirements: PhD student or postdoc status. Original research.

https://www.cameo3d.org/.title: CAMEO – Continuous Automated Model Evaluation
https://www.cameo3d.org/.description: Continuous evaluation service for protein structure prediction methods. Weekly targets from PDB release.
https://www.cameo3d.org/.how_to_apply: Register server, submit predictions weekly via API or web interface.
https://www.cameo3d.org/.requirements: Automated prediction pipeline. Server must be registered.

https://alphafold.ebi.ac.uk/.title: AlphaFold Protein Structure Database Challenge
https://alphafold.ebi.ac.uk/.description: Community challenge to predict structures for proteins in AlphaFold DB. Compare your method against state-of-the-art.
https://alphafold.ebi.ac.uk/.how_to_apply: Download sequences from AFDB, submit predictions, upload to evaluation server.
https://alphafold.ebi.ac.uk/.requirements: Predictions in PDB format. Method documentation required.

https://biodesignchallenge.org/.title: Biodesign Challenge 2025
https://biodesignchallenge.org/.prizes: Exhibition at Museum of Modern Art + Awards
https://biodesignchallenge.org/.description: International competition combining design and synthetic biology. Teams envision future biotechnological applications.
https://biodesignchallenge.org/.how_to_apply: University registers, forms interdisciplinary team, submits concept.
https://biodesignchallenge.org/.requirements: Design concept, prototypes, presentation at finals.

https://www.pdbj.org/csa/.title: PDBj Coding Sprint for Algorithms
https://www.pdbj.org/csa/.description: Algorithm development sprint focused on PDB data mining, visualization, and analysis tools.
https://www.pdbj.org/csa/.how_to_apply: Submit CV and project proposal through PDBj portal.
https://www.pdbj.org/csa/.requirements: Programming skills (Python/Java). Interest in structural biology.

https://rosie.rosettacommons.org/.title: ROSIE – Rosetta Online Server Evaluation
https://rosie.rosettacommons.org/.description: Continuous evaluation platform for Rosetta-based protein design and structure prediction methods.
https://rosie.rosettacommons.org/.how_to_apply: Register on ROSIE, submit method for blind testing against weekly targets.
https://rosie.rosettacommons.org/.requirements: Rosetta protocol or custom method. Documentation required.

https://www.ebi.ac.uk/pdbe/emdb/.title: Cryo-EM Model Challenge
https://www.ebi.ac.uk/pdbe/emdb/.description: Challenge to build atomic models from cryo-EM density maps. Tests modeling methods for membrane proteins and complexes.
https://www.ebi.ac.uk/pdbe/emdb/.how_to_apply: Download maps from EMDB, submit models, participate in validation.

https://sbgrid.org/collaborations/.title: SBGrid Software Challenge
https://sbgrid.org/collaborations/.description: Challenge to develop and optimize open-source software tools for structural biology.
https://sbgrid.org/collaborations/.how_to_apply: Submit software proposal through SBGrid portal. Code must be open source.
https://sbgrid.org/collaborations/.requirements: Open source license. Documentation and tests required.
